note_data.title: Major Themes of the Quran
keyPoints: Allah is the one and only God, with no god but He, the living, the self-subsisting, eternal
No slumber can seize Him nor sleep, and His are all things in the heavens and on earth
He created all things and has full knowledge of all things, being the Creator of all things
No vision can grasp Him, but His grasp is over all vision: He is above all comprehension, yet is acquainted with all things
To Him is due the primal origin of the heavens and the earth, and He has power to dispose of all affairs
Allah is most gracious, most merciful, the oft-forgiving, and Master of the day of judgement
Among His signs are the night and the day, and the sun and the moon - adore Allah who created them
He made the earth your couch, and the heavens your canopy; and sent down rain from the heavens to bring forth fruits for sustenance
He who taught by the pen, taught man what he did not know
Allah created a vicegerent on earth and taught Adam the names of all things

definitions.definition: Allah is eternal and depends on nothing else for His existence
A representative or deputy - referring to humans as Allah's representatives on earth
Allah as the protector and sustainer who created humans and provides for them
Allah's position above all creation, feeling no fatigue in guarding and preserving all things
The path of those to whom Allah has given His grace, not those who earn His anger or go astray

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one theme in one sentence. Example: 'Tawhid is a major theme of the Quran.'
Name the theme + explain its importance using 'because' or 'this shows that'
Give multiple themes with detailed explanations and conclude which is most important

modelAnswer.question: Explain why Tawhid is considered the most important theme of the Quran. [4 marks]
modelAnswer.answer: Tawhid is the most important theme because it is the foundation of Islamic belief that Allah is One and Unique. This is shown in Surah Al-Ikhlas which declares 'Say He is Allah, the One' emphasizing His absolute unity. Tawhid is important because it establishes that Allah alone deserves worship and has no partners or associates. The Quran repeatedly emphasizes this theme because shirk (associating partners with Allah) is the greatest sin, making Tawhid essential for salvation.
markBreakdown: Mark 1: Tawhid means Allah is One and Unique
Mark 2: Reference to Surah Al-Ikhlas or similar
Mark 3: Explanation that Allah alone deserves worship
Mark 4: Connection to shirk being the greatest sin

commonMistakes: Setting up rivals to Allah when you know He is the only Creator
Adorning the sun and moon instead of Allah who created them
Not recognizing that Allah has no consort and does not beget nor is He begotten
Failing to understand that Allah's knowledge encompasses what is before, after and behind all things
Not acknowledging that intercession in Allah's presence is only as He permits
examinerTips: When explaining passages, briefly identify the main theme(s) in each passage for 4 marks
Explain the importance of these themes in a Muslim's life today for another 4 marks
Choose any two passages from the given options to demonstrate understanding of Quranic themes
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: No specific diagram required, but a mind map showing the major themes (Tawhid at center, connected to Risalah, Akhirah, Justice, Worship, Moral Values) with key Quranic references for each theme
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2023 P2 Q1 (4 marks) — they asked students to outline the Muslim teaching about belief in the oneness of God (tawhid)
This topic was tested in O/N 2022 P1 Q2 (6 marks) — they asked students to state how tawhid benefits Muslims in everyday life and give reasons why without tawhid there is no faith
This topic was tested in M/J 2024 P2 Q3 (8 marks) — they asked students to write about the three main categories of shirk (associating partners with God)
This topic was tested in O/N 2023 P1 Q4 (10 marks) — they asked students to explain the significance of prophetic revelations in the Quran
This topic was tested in M/J 2022 P2 Q5 (6 marks) — they asked students to describe the Quranic teachings about the Day of Judgment and its importance

workedExample.question: Outline the Muslim teaching about belief in the oneness of God (tawhid). [4 marks]
steps: Step 1: Define tawhid as the fundamental Islamic belief in the absolute oneness and uniqueness of Allah
Step 2: Explain that Allah has no partners, equals, or associates in His divinity
Step 3: Mention that tawhid includes belief in Allah's unique attributes and His exclusive right to worship
Step 4: Reference Quranic evidence such as Surah Al-Ikhlas which declares Allah's absolute oneness
workedExample.answer: Tawhid is the fundamental Islamic belief in the absolute oneness and uniqueness of Allah. Muslims believe that Allah is one, indivisible, and has no partners, equals, or associates in His divinity. This concept encompasses belief in Allah's unique attributes, His exclusive right to worship, and His supreme authority over all creation. The Quran emphasizes this in Surah Al-Ikhlas: 'Say: He is Allah, the One! Allah, the Eternal, Absolute; He begets not, nor is He begotten; And there is none like unto Him.'
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for defining tawhid, 1 mark for explaining Allah's uniqueness, 1 mark for mentioning exclusive worship, 1 mark for Quranic reference or example

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates confused the themes of the Quran with the themes of Hadith' — always specify that you are discussing Quranic teachings and provide Surah references when possible

Examiner Report 2022: 'Candidates often gave vague answers about tawhid without explaining its practical implications' — always connect theological concepts to how they affect Muslim daily life and worship

Examiner Report 2024: 'Students frequently mixed up the categories of shirk or gave incomplete explanations' — ensure you can clearly distinguish between major shirk (al-shirk al-akbar), minor shirk (al-shirk al-asghar), and hidden shirk (al-shirk al-khafi)
note_data.keyDiagram: Create a mind map with 'Major Quranic Themes' at the center, with six main branches: 1) Tawhid (Oneness of God) - include sub-branches for Allah's attributes, worship, and rejection of shirk; 2) Prophethood - showing the chain from Adam to Muhammad (PBUH); 3) Revelation - connecting previous scriptures to the Quran; 4) Akhirah (Afterlife) - showing Day of Judgment, Paradise, and Hell; 5) Social Justice - including charity, equality, and community responsibilities; 6) Moral Guidance - covering personal conduct, family life, and ethical principles. Use different colors for each theme and include key Quranic verses as supporting evidence under each branch.
note_data.quickRecap: The Quran presents major themes centered on Allah's oneness, His attributes as Creator and Sustainer, and His relationship with creation. Key passages emphasize Allah's eternal nature, His knowledge and power over all things, and His role as the most gracious and merciful. These themes guide Muslims in worship, understanding their purpose as vicegerents on earth, and seeking Allah's guidance on the straight path.
